---
title: "Using AI to Analyze and Strengthen Your Business"
subtitle: "Capital Readiness Program for Women Entrepreneurs"
author: "Dr. Logan Kelly · Small Business AI Center"
institute: "Small Business AI Center"
date: "April 14, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [white, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    footer: "Dr. Logan Kelly | UWRF"
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    embed-resources: true
---

## Today's Plan

1. **C.O.R.E. Framework** — A structured way to get useful output from AI
2. **Live Demo** — C.O.R.E. on real business data (twice)
3. **Three Things to Try This Week**

::: {.notes}
~30 sec. Set expectations: short on theory, heavy on demo.
:::

---

## The C.O.R.E. Framework

| Component | What It Means | What You Do |
|-----------|--------------|-------------|
| **C**ontext | Situational awareness | Feed AI your actual business data |
| **O**bjective | Strategic intent | Tell AI exactly what you want |
| **R**easoning | Multi-step logic | Make AI show its work |
| **E**valuation | Risk & validation | Ask AI to critique itself |

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026). "How FP&A Professionals Should Prompt AI in 2026." *FP&A Trends*.
:::

::: {.notes}
~2 min. This is THE slide. Everything else is just unpacking these four letters.
:::

---

## C + O — Context and Objective

**Context:** Upload your **actual business files** — invoices, reports, records.
Modern AI can process thousands of rows instantly.

**Objective — use the D.A.T.A. pattern for sharp goals:**

| | |
|---|---|
| **D**ata Source | "Use the January invoice export" |
| **A**ction | "Identify top 5 late-paying industries" |
| **T**echnical Constraints | "Ignore credit memos" |
| **A**nswer Style | "Return a ranked table" |

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026); Stellium Consulting (2026).
:::

::: {.notes}
~2 min. Context = ground truth. Objective = sharp goal. Mention only 1% of companies are "AI mature" (McKinsey 2025).
:::

---

## R + E — Reasoning and Evaluation

**Reasoning:** Prompt **"Think step-by-step"** — forces auditable logic and
reduces hallucinations. Shifts AI from template filler to reasoning partner.

**Evaluation:** Prompt **"Critique your own analysis. What are the limitations?"**
Human-in-the-loop is **non-negotiable**.

::: {.callout-warning appearance="simple"}
**Cautionary tale:** Deloitte Australia refunded part of an AU$440,000 contract —
AI-generated report contained fabricated court quotes.
:::

::: {.aside}
Jasimuddin (2026); Probert (2026).
:::

::: {.notes}
~2 min. Deloitte story lands the point about Evaluation — even pros get burned without verification.
:::

---

## Three AI Risks to Know

| Risk | Key Fact |
|------|---------|
| **Hallucinations** | Deloitte case — fabricated court quotes (Probert, 2026) |
| **Data leakage** | 68% of organizations affected (SentinelOne, 2026) |
| **Model bias** | 70% of firms unprepared for biased outputs (SentinelOne, 2026) |

**The fix is the same in every case: human-in-the-loop verification.**

::: {.notes}
~1.5 min. Don't dwell — point is: these risks are why Evaluation is in C.O.R.E.
:::

# Live Demo {.section-header}

---

## Demo: Invoice Collections Analysis

**Walk through C.O.R.E. on a real billing dataset**

- ~50,000 billing transactions
- One combined C.O.R.E. prompt — watch for the four XML tags
- Question: which industries pay late?

::: {.notes}
~8 min including narration. Open the prompts.md file. Read the prompt aloud, hit submit, narrate output.
:::

---

## Demo: Cash Flow Forecasting

**Same pattern, different data — cash inflows**

- Small business financial projections template
- All four C.O.R.E. steps in a single prompt
- Scenario: what if revenue drops 20% in Q3?

::: {.notes}
~7 min. Different data, same prompt shape. That's the whole point.
:::

# Your Move {.section-header}

---

## Three Things to Try This Week

1. **Anonymize a file and run a C.O.R.E. prompt**
   Remove names/IDs, upload to ChatGPT or Claude.

2. **Add a self-critique prompt to any AI output**
   "Audit this for errors or unsupported assumptions."

3. **Document your AI tools**
   List every AI tool and dataset your business uses today.

::: {.notes}
~1.5 min. These are in the handout. Read each one out loud.
:::

---

## Resources & Contact

**Repo & downloads:** [github.com/LJKelly3141/crp-webinar-ai](https://github.com/LJKelly3141/crp-webinar-ai)

Slides · Handout · Invoice data · Data dictionary · Financial template

---

**Logan Kelly, Ph.D.**

| | |
|---|---|
| Codirector, Small Business AI Clinic | Professor of Economics |
| Universities of Wisconsin · OBE | UW–River Falls · SBE |
| logan.kelly@business.wisconsin.edu | logan.kelly@uwrf.edu |
| 715-425-3335 | 715-425-3335 |

::: {.notes}
~1 min. Point to the handout for full reference list and "level up your toolkit" suggestions.
:::

---

## Q&A {.center}

Thank you!
